caption: @Alix Earle you have good taste! #katami #sushi #houstoneats #houstonfood #hungryhay
transcript: When Alex Earl said her favorite sushi restaurant was in my hometown, I had to make a stop. This is Katami, which is the sister restaurant to my favorite sushi place, Katarabata. We started off with miso soup, which had the most amazing homemade broth and some oysters with caviar. We also got the Shishito peppers, which had such a great flavor, but the Toro tartar really stole the show. They served the fatty tuna with milk bread, which I had never had before, but it was amazing. After appetizers, we moved on to Nagiri, starting off with the fatty salmon and a Toro, and then the scallops and the wagyu. We ended up getting two more of the wagyu. It was that good. We also got the Texas Hamachi roll, which is our favorite roll at Katarabata. We finished our dinner with the wagyu hot rock and the miso lobster. I had no doubt that this dinner would be amazing, but wow, Chef Tommy and his team exceeded our expectations. Definitely add Katami to your list if you're visiting Houston soon.
address: 2701 W Dallas St, Houston, TX 77019, USA

caption: Celebrating 4 months of marriage at @stanfordshoppingcenter 💛 Sushi Roku made the perfect date night!  #sponsored #SimonMalls #StanfordShoppingCenter #datenight #anniversarydinner #anniversary #sushidate
transcript: We're having date night to celebrate our four-month wedding anniversary, so we came to sushi roku for the first time. And honestly, it was such a great choice. The atmosphere was just beautiful. The lighting and the music made it feel really cosy, and the service was just amazing. Everything tasted so fresh and flavorful, and the drinks were light, refreshing, and paired perfectly with the food. The whole experience felt elevated, but still relaxed, and it's definitely somewhere we'd like to come back to again. After dinner, we stopped by the new skim stores, and they just opened. The space was clean and minimal, and they had so many cute new pieces in stock. I grabbed a few things to try, and everything fit so well. We also popped into Cartier for a bit before ending the night at La D'Arac for something sweet. We picked out a few chocolate pieces to share, and it was the perfect little treat to end the night.
address: 405 Colorado St Suite 100, Austin, TX 78701, USA

caption: Texas Restaurants Aga’s and Taste of Texas #agas #texas #houstontexas #houstonfoodie #restaurant @Aga’s @Taste of Texas
transcript: When you go to Houston, Texas, there are two restaurants you have to try. One of them being August. This is the most popular Indian restaurant in the country. You know I had to try that mango lassi and oof, the texture, the flavor, the sweetness. It was so perfect. We also got some delicious samostas. I preferred the potato one over the beef one. The chicken lollipops would try a nice cake to it as well as crispy and tender. This is a must. But nothing beats these tender, juicy packed with flavor lamb chops. I mean, wow. The chicken bbiyani was very flavorful as well, but I couldn't leave without getting that butter chicken. It was phenomenal, especially paired with their soft garlic non absolute must. And oof, we had to end it off with this galab jamun. So sweet, soft, the best one I've ever had. 9.6 for August. Another restaurant that's a must is Taste of Texas. I loved everything about this place. First of all, this place is gigantic, just like their steak. Their breads are so good. If you're going to have Texas rolls, let it be in Texas. For apps, we got this brea. It's nothing crazy. Save the space for the giant steaks instead. Speaking of steak, we got to go to the back and pick out the exact steak that we wanted. I did a 16 ounce ribeye, which I loved. Their meat is so high quality. Tender, juicy. I enjoyed this so much. We also got the porterhouse, which is 32 ounces of beauty. And my favorite was the cowboy, which was on a whole other level. We also got sides, potato, or grottin and baked potato, which were both good. As for dessert, I loved how they showed it off on this platter. We got their most popular one, which is like the Snickers cake. But honestly, I would just skip dessert overall. 9.4 for Taste of Texas. That brings my Texas journey to an end. If you want to see my other Texas videos on food, check out my Texas playlist.

caption: The newest addition to River Oaks District did NOT disappoint🍝🥂✨ #italian #riveroaksdistrict #newrestaurant #houstonfood #htxeats
transcript: Last night we went to the New Italian restaurant in River Oaks District and let me just say I will 100% be coming back. It's called barry and is so worth a stop. If this vibe wasn't already convincing enough, the food and drinks exceeded our expectations. I mean, look at the caraph our bottle of wine came in. To start, we had the Frito Misto and a dozen oysters which were both so good. We also got the Caesar salad which wasn't my personal favorite but the presentation was a ten out of ten. For mains, I got the spinach and ricotta ravioli which was cooked in this brown butter and sage sauce. But the star of the show was definitely this bolognese. Oh my god. Definitely recommend checking it out. This place is for sure a new favorite.
address: 4444 Westheimer Rd suite a175, Houston, TX 77027, USA
